chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to launch the Rheonics Control Panel application, showing a screenshot of its location within a system menu.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Running the RCP Program

Modified on: Fri, 3 Jul, 2020 at 12:18 PM

Open the Rheonics Control Panel application from the Start menu or from its Desktop icon, if created during the installation process.

Rheonics System Suite
Rheonics Control Panel
Rheonics Control Panel
Back
Search programs and files
Sleep

When the software is running, you will see a screen similar to the following:
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel application can be launched from the Start menu or a Desktop icon.
A Desktop icon for the Rheonics Control Panel may be created during the installation process.
Upon successful execution, the software will display a specific interface (which is not depicted on this page).

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to establish a USB communication connection between the Rheonics Control Panel software and an SME sensor, detailing the initial 'not connected' status and configuration steps.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics Control Panel
Measurement Events Settings Service Comm Configure Help

Measured Values
Viscosity: not connected
Density: not connected
Temperature: not connected
Pressure: not connected

Status
Error: not connected E99
Logging: Stopped
Pressure: Manual
Manual entry (checkbox) 1 bar

Log Measurements
Sample Name: (empty text field)
Start Logging (button)
Add Event (button)

Sensor Information
Serial Number: -no sn-
Fres: -
dF: -

Graph Labels (top to bottom on Y-axis):
Viscosity [mPa.s]
Density [g/cc]
Temperature [°C]
Pressure [bar]

Graph Labels (X-axis):
hh:mm:ss
Time Range: 00:00:00 to 00:01:49 (for two visible graphs)

Notice that the “Status” indicator will probably be colored red, indicating that the SME is not connected.

Establishing communications between RCP Software and the SME

USB connection

Power up the SME by connecting a suitable power supply to the power input terminal block.
Make sure the USB cable is plugged into the micro-USB port on SRV-SME and into a free USB port on your computer.

1. Select the “Settings” tab and select the “USB” button under the “Communication” panel:

Rheonics Control Panel
Measurement Events Settings Service Diagnostics Help

Operator
Name / ID: Rheonics
Client: Rheonics GmbH

Communication
USB (radio button, selected)
Port: COM1 (dropdown)
Device Manager (button)
Status (red dot)
Apply (button)
Ethernet (radio button)
IP: 192.168.1.1
Port: 10001
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software displays real-time data for Viscosity, Density, Temperature, and Pressure.
The initial status of an SME not being connected is indicated by a red 'Error: not connected E99' message in the software.
To establish USB communication, the SME must be powered up via its power input terminal block and connected to a computer via a micro-USB to USB cable.
Communication settings for the SME, including USB and Ethernet options, are configured within the 'Settings' tab of the Rheonics Control Panel software.
The software provides fields for logging measurements, including a 'Sample Name' and buttons for 'Start Logging' and 'Add Event'.
warnings_notes: Notice that the “Status” indicator will probably be colored red, indicating that the SME is not connected.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions on how to identify and select the correct COM port for a Rheonics device's Electronics Box using the Windows Device Manager and the Rheonics Control Panel.
chunks.extracted_text:
2. Click on the “Device Manager” button to open the Device Manager.

[Screenshot: Rheonics Control Panel]
Tabs: Measurement, Events, Settings, Service, Diagnostics, Help
Operator:
  Name / ID: Rheonics
  Client: Rheonics GmbH
Communication:
  Radio: USB (selected)
  Port: COM1 (dropdown)
  Button: Device Manager
  Status: (red dot)
  Button: Apply (grayed out)
  Radio: Ethernet
  IP: 192.168.1.1
  Port: 10001

3. Navigate to “Ports (COM & LPT) tab and expand it.

[Screenshot: Device Manager]
Window Title: Device Manager
Menu: File, Action, View, Help
Device Tree:
  > rheo1
  > Audio inputs and outputs
  > Computer
  > Disk drives
  > Display adapters
  > DVD/CD-ROM drives
  > Human Interface Devices
  > Imaging devices
  > Keyboards
  > Mice and other pointing devices
  > Monitors
  > Multifunction adapters
  > Network adapters
  v Ports (COM & LPT)
    Communications Port (COM1)
    ECP Printer Port (LPT1)
    Silicon Labs CP210x USB to UART Bridge (COM5)
    SME (COM6)
    SUNIX COM Port (COM3)
    SUNIX COM Port (COM4)
  > Print queues
  > Printers
  > Processors
  > Software devices
  > Sound, video and game controllers
  > Storage controllers

The interface used by the Electronics Box is labeled “SME” and is here assigned to Port COM6. Make a note of the COM port number and close the Device Manager.

4. Select the COM Port you discovered (COM6 in this example) from the drop-down list.
key_facts: The 'Device Manager' button within the Rheonics Control Panel is used to access the Windows Device Manager.
The Rheonics Control Panel facilitates communication setup via USB or Ethernet.
To identify the correct COM port for the Rheonics Electronics Box, navigate to 'Ports (COM & LPT)' in the Windows Device Manager.
The interface for the Rheonics Electronics Box is identified as 'SME' in the Device Manager.
The example shows 'SME' being assigned to 'COM6'.
The identified COM port must then be selected in the Rheonics Control Panel's communication settings.
diagram_labels: Silicon Labs CP210x USB to UART Bridge (COM5)

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a step-by-step guide on how to establish a USB communication connection using the Rheonics Control Panel software and verify its status.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics Control Panel
Tabs: Measurement, Events, Settings, Service, Diagnostics, Help

Operator:
  Name / ID: Rheonics
  Client: Rheonics GmbH

Communication:
  Radio button: USB (selected)
  Radio button: Ethernet
  Port (dropdown with options): COM1, COM2, COM3, COM4, COM5, COM6 (selected)
  Refresh button
  IP: [empty text field]
  Port: 10001 (text field)
  Device Manager button
  Status: [Red circle indicator]
  Apply button

Sensor Configuration:
  Sensor Type: [empty text field]
  Import button
  Export button
  Display button

Units:
  Viscosity: [unselected radio button] cSt
  Density: [selected radio button] g/cc, [unselected radio button] kg/m³, [unselected radio button] lb/gal, [unselected radio button] lb/ft³
  Temperature: [selected radio button] °C, [unselected radio button] °K, [unselected radio button] °F

Check Sensor button

5. Press the “Apply” button to start the connection.

[Screenshot showing Rheonics Control Panel with 'Settings' tab and 'Apply' button highlighted, Status indicator is red.]

6. Wait until the “Status” indicator turns to green.

[Screenshot showing Rheonics Control Panel with 'Status' indicator green.]

The communication over USB is now established and the configuration and measurement values from the SME are displayed on the Rheonics Control Panel.
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software is used for configuring and monitoring Rheonics devices.
Communication with the device can be established via USB or Ethernet.
The 'Apply' button initiates the connection process.
A red status indicator signifies no active connection, while a green indicator signifies an established connection.
Once communication is established, configuration and measurement values from the SME (Sensor Measurement Engine) are displayed in the Control Panel.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to establish an Ethernet connection for Rheonics devices, featuring a screenshot of the Rheonics Control Panel software displaying real-time measured values and sensor information.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics Control Panel
Measurement | Events | Settings | Service | Comm Configure | Help

Measured Values
Viscosity 0.00 mPa.s
Density 0.0100 g/cc
Temperature 25.08 °C
Pressure 1.00 bar

Status
Running... E00
Logging Stopped
Pressure Manual
[✓] Manual entry 1 bar

Log Measurements
Sample Name
Start Logging | Add Event

Sensor Information
Serial Number B05-0163
Fres 7473.42 Hz
dF 1.04 Hz

Ethernet connection
This section applies only to devices with Ethernet enabled and describes how to connect the SME via Ethernet to the Rheonics Control Panel. If your device does not have the Ethernet enabled, consult your sales representative for information on ordering this option.

Connecting the Electronics Box to your network (DHCP)
* Connect the Electronics Box to your network with an Ethernet cable
* Power on the Electronics Box
* The Electronics Box is set by default to obtain an IP-Address from the network (DHCP)

Detect the electronics box on the network
You LAN switch should be consulted to get the correct IP and MAC address for the SME electronics.

Enter the IP-Address on Rheonics Control Panel
1. Start Rheonics Control Panel
key_facts: This section on Ethernet connection is only applicable to devices with Ethernet enabled.
The SME connects to the Rheonics Control Panel via Ethernet.
The Electronics Box connects to the network using an Ethernet cable.
The Electronics Box is configured by default to obtain an IP-Address from the network via DHCP.
A LAN switch should be consulted to obtain the correct IP and MAC address for the SME electronics.
The first step to enter the IP-Address is to start the Rheonics Control Panel.
warnings_notes: If your device does not have the Ethernet enabled, consult your sales representative for information on ordering this option.
Ethernet cable must be plugged in before powering on the Electronics Box.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions on how to establish an Ethernet communication link with SME electronics using the Rheonics Control Panel software.
chunks.extracted_text:
2. Open tab “Settings” and select the radio button “Ethernet”

[Screenshot of Rheonics Control Panel showing 'Settings' tab selected. The 'Communication' section has 'Ethernet' radio button selected, 'IP' set to '192.168.1.1', 'Port' set to '10001', and 'Status' indicator is red.]

Select “Ethernet” on Communication section

[Screenshot of Rheonics Control Panel showing 'Settings' tab selected. The 'Communication' section has 'Ethernet' radio button selected, 'IP' field containing '10.5.33.58' is highlighted, 'Port' set to '10001', and 'Status' indicator is red.]

Enter the IP-Address of the SME electronics

[Screenshot of Rheonics Control Panel showing 'Settings' tab selected. The 'Communication' section has 'Ethernet' radio button selected, 'IP' field containing '10.5.33.58' is highlighted, 'Port' set to '10001', and 'Status' indicator is red.]

Press the “Apply” button to establish the connection and wait until the “Status” indicator turns green.

[Screenshot of Rheonics Control Panel showing 'Settings' tab selected. The 'Communication' section has 'Ethernet' radio button selected, 'IP' set to '10.5.33.58', 'Port' set to '10001'. The 'Apply' button is highlighted, and the 'Status' indicator is green and highlighted.]

The communication over Ethernet is now established and the measurement values from the SME are displayed on the Rheonics Control Panel.
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software is used to configure communication with SME electronics.
Ethernet communication is enabled by navigating to the 'Settings' tab and selecting the 'Ethernet' radio button.
The specific IP address of the SME electronics must be entered into the 'IP' field.
Pressing the 'Apply' button initiates the connection attempt.
A successful Ethernet connection is confirmed when the 'Status' indicator changes from red to green.
Once connected, the Rheonics Control Panel displays measurement values received from the SME.

chunks.page_summary: This page displays a screenshot of the Rheonics Control Panel software interface showing real-time measurements and sensor information, accompanied by instructions on how the software loads and verifies sensor configuration parameters.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics Control Panel
Measurement Events Settings Service Comm Configure Help

Measured Values
Viscosity 0.00 mPa.s
Density 0.0100 g/cc
Temperature 25.07 °C
Pressure 1.00 bar

Status
(Green indicator) Running... E00
Logging Stopped
Pressure Manual
(Checked) Manual entry 1 bar

Log Measurements
Sample Name [Input Field]
Start Logging [Button] Add Event [Button]

Sensor Information
Serial Number B05-0163
Fres 7473.46 Hz
dF 1.02 Hz

Graph 1 (Top Graph):
Y-axis Left: Viscosity [mPa.s] (Range: -1.00 to 1.00)
Y-axis Right: Density [g/cc] (Range: -0.0110 to -0.0090)
X-axis: hh:mm:ss (Time from 00:34:51 to 01:08:10)

Graph 2 (Bottom Graph):
Y-axis Left: Temperature [°C] (Range: 25.02 to 25.08)
Y-axis Right: Pressure [bar] (Range: -1.10 to -0.90)
X-axis: hh:mm:ss (Time from 00:34:51 to 01:08:10)

Loading the configuration parameters
The Rheonics Control Panel automatically loads the configuration for the specific sensor that you are using. Once the connection between the RCP and the SME is established, the RCP will automatically load the configuration parameters from SME.
To check that the configuration parameters are loaded go to the “Service” Tab and verify that the “System Info” panel shows the correct sensor serial number (S/N). A unique sensor serial number is printed on each Rheonics sensor.
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) automatically loads the configuration for the specific sensor in use.
Configuration parameters are automatically loaded from the SME once a connection between the RCP and SME is established.
Users should verify that the correct sensor serial number (S/N) is loaded by checking the 'System Info' panel under the 'Service' tab.
Each Rheonics sensor has a unique serial number printed on it.

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software is used to configure and monitor Rheonics inline viscometers and density sensors.
The software provides sections for system information, software versions, 4-20 mA output settings, 4-20 mA calibration checks, and density configuration.
Users can define 4-20 mA output ranges for viscosity (mPa.s), density (g/cc), and temperature (°C) for three separate channels.
The 4-20 mA Calibration Check feature allows testing and calibrating output signals for each channel at 4 mA, 16 mA, and 10 mA.
Density for SRV sensors can be set as a constant value or defined by a temperature-dependent quadratic curve.
A "Refresh" button is available to update or load values, particularly in the "Service" tab as indicated by the error message.
Disconnection of the SMET (Sensor Measurement Electronics or similar module) prevents the loading of configuration parameters and displays an error.
warnings_notes: If there was a problem loading the configuration parameters due to disconnection of the SME, you will see the following warning in the RCP:
The SMET is not connected. The configurations parameters could not be loaded. Please, connect the SMET, and get the configuration parameters by clicking "Refresh"in "Service" Tab

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an instruction on how to load or refresh configuration parameters by using the 'Refresh' button in the 'Service' Tab.
chunks.extracted_text: You can attempt to load or refresh the configuration parameters by clicking the “Refresh” button in “Service” Tab.
key_facts: Configuration parameters can be loaded or refreshed.
To refresh or load parameters, click the 'Refresh' button.
The 'Refresh' button is located within the 'Service' Tab.
